text: The video demonstrates the use of near-infrared laparoscopic fluorescence during total mesorectal excision for rectal cancer. Fluorescent areas are visible at the base of the mesocolon, indicating lymph nodes along the superior rectal artery. The Pinpoint system provides augmented reality views, switching between white light and near-infrared imaging to enhance visualization of lymphatic channels and nodes.

In a sixty-two-year-old patient with rectal cancer, the surgeon performs a total mesorectal excision. The video shows the posterior and lateral dissection of the mesorectum, utilizing near-infrared (NIR) fluorescence to visualize lymphatic channels and lymph nodes. Green fluorescent points corresponding to mesorectal lymph nodes are visible as the mesorectum is mobilized forward. The dissection requires careful differentiation between visceral and parietal fascia due to secondary lymphatic uptake within the retroperitoneum.

During the dissection, lymphatic channels draining the rectum are clearly visible, along with specific fluorescent points representing nodes within the mesorectum. The near-infrared fluorescence laparoscopy aids in visualizing these structures, highlighting the embryological planes necessary for effective oncological resection.
